title: Friday, 07/08/2026 Quiz: The August Mix
description: A Chase-style Friday quiz covering movies and television, pop culture, geography, and light current world politics. The rounds aim for broad knowledge with a few satisfying stretch questions. Political facts were checked on 17 July 2026 and should be rechecked before hosting.

questions.question: Which film won Best Picture at the 2024 Academy Awards?
questions.notes: Oppenheimer won seven Oscars, including Best Picture and Best Director.

questions.question: In the television series Severance, what is the name of the mysterious company where Mark Scout works?
questions.notes: Accept Lumon or Lumon Industries.

questions.question: Which director made Jaws, Jurassic Park and Schindler's List?
questions.notes: All three films were directed by Steven Spielberg.

questions.question: The first season of The White Lotus is set at a resort in which island group?
questions.notes: The first season is set in Hawaii; the second moves to Sicily.

questions.question: Who played FBI trainee Clarice Starling in The Silence of the Lambs?
questions.notes: Jodie Foster won the Academy Award for Best Actress for the role. Julianne Moore played Clarice in the sequel Hannibal.

questions.question: Which television series is set mainly on the fictional continents of Westeros and Essos?
questions.notes: Game of Thrones is the intended answer. Do not require the book-series title A Song of Ice and Fire.

questions.question: Which film became the first primarily non-English-language movie to win the Academy Award for Best Picture?
questions.notes: South Korea's Parasite made history at the 2020 Academy Awards.

questions.question: What is the name of the media conglomerate controlled by the Roy family in Succession?
questions.notes: Accept Waystar Royco or simply Waystar. Pierce Global Media is its rival.

questions.question: Which New Zealand actor plays Homelander in The Boys?
questions.notes: Antony Starr plays Homelander. Accept the common misspelling Anthony Starr. Karl Urban plays Billy Butcher.

questions.question: The line 'I see dead people' comes from which film?
questions.notes: The line is spoken by Cole Sear in The Sixth Sense.

questions.question: Stefani Germanotta is the real name of which pop star?
questions.notes: Lady Gaga was born Stefani Joanne Angelina Germanotta.

questions.question: Which artist became the first person to win the Grammy for Album of the Year four times?
questions.notes: Taylor Swift reached four wins with Midnights in 2024.

questions.question: Which band released the album Rumours?
questions.notes: Fleetwood Mac released Rumours in 1977.

questions.question: Sasha Fierce is the stage alter ego of which performer?
questions.notes: The persona appears in the title of Beyoncé's 2008 album I Am... Sasha Fierce.

questions.question: What was the first music video played when MTV launched in 1981?
questions.notes: Video Killed the Radio Star by the Buggles was MTV's first video.

questions.question: Abel Tesfaye performs under which stage name?
questions.notes: Accept The Weeknd or Weeknd.

questions.question: Which singer released the 2001 debut album Songs in A Minor?
questions.notes: Songs in A Minor was Alicia Keys' debut studio album.

questions.question: Melanie Chisholm was better known by which Spice Girls nickname?
questions.notes: Melanie Chisholm, also known as Mel C, was Sporty Spice.

questions.question: Which singer founded the Fenty Beauty cosmetics brand?
questions.notes: Fenty is Rihanna's surname; her full name is Robyn Rihanna Fenty.

questions.question: Nemo won the 2024 Eurovision Song Contest while representing which country?
questions.notes: Nemo represented Switzerland with the song The Code.

questions.question: The ancient pyramids of Meroë are found in which modern country?
questions.notes: Meroë was a major city of the Kingdom of Kush in present-day Sudan.

questions.question: Which four national capital cities does the River Danube flow through?
questions.notes: Award one point each for Vienna, Bratislava, Budapest and Belgrade.

questions.question: What is the largest island in the Mediterranean Sea?
questions.notes: Sicily is the Mediterranean's largest island by area.

questions.question: Which city is Bolivia's constitutional capital, even though the government sits in La Paz?
questions.notes: Sucre is the constitutional capital; La Paz is the administrative seat of government.

questions.question: Through Gibraltar, the United Kingdom has a land border with which country?
questions.notes: The British Overseas Territory of Gibraltar borders Spain.

questions.question: Which sea is defined by ocean currents rather than by surrounding land?
questions.notes: The Sargasso Sea lies in the North Atlantic and has no land boundaries.

questions.question: Which present-day African country was historically known as Abyssinia?
questions.notes: Abyssinia is the historical European name commonly associated with Ethiopia.

questions.question: Which capital city stands near the meeting point of the Blue Nile and White Nile?
questions.notes: The two branches of the Nile meet at Khartoum in Sudan.

questions.question: Which two countries share the Caribbean island of Hispaniola?
questions.notes: Award one point each for Haiti and the Dominican Republic.

questions.question: What is the world's deepest lake?
questions.notes: Lake Baikal in Siberia is the world's deepest lake.

questions.question: Who became Germany's federal chancellor in May 2025?
questions.notes: Friedrich Merz became federal chancellor on 6 May 2025. Current as checked on 17 July 2026.

questions.question: Which party does Australian prime minister Anthony Albanese lead?
questions.notes: Note the Australian spelling Labor, without the letter u. Current as checked on 17 July 2026.

questions.question: Before becoming NATO secretary general, Mark Rutte was prime minister of which country?
questions.notes: Mark Rutte was prime minister of the Netherlands from 2010 to 2024. Accept Holland in a friendly quiz. Current role checked on 17 July 2026.

questions.question: Before entering Canadian politics, Mark Carney served as governor of which two central banks?
questions.notes: Award one point each for the Bank of Canada and Bank of England.

questions.question: Before becoming president of Mexico, Claudia Sheinbaum led the government of which major city?
questions.notes: Sheinbaum served as Head of Government of Mexico City from 2018 to 2023.

questions.question: Who replaced Winston Peters as New Zealand's deputy prime minister in May 2025 under the coalition's power-sharing agreement?
questions.notes: David Seymour took over the role on 31 May 2025. Current as checked on 17 July 2026.

questions.question: Ursula von der Leyen is president of which European Union institution?
questions.notes: Do not accept European Council; that is a separate EU institution. Current as checked on 17 July 2026.

questions.question: Which amendment to the US Constitution limits a president to being elected twice?
questions.notes: The Twenty-Second Amendment was ratified in 1951.

questions.question: Which South American president became famous for using a chainsaw as a campaign symbol?
questions.notes: Argentina's Javier Milei used a chainsaw to symbolise planned cuts to government spending.

questions.question: Which current European president previously played a fictional president in the television comedy Servant of the People?
questions.notes: Volodymyr Zelenskyy starred in Servant of the People before becoming president of Ukraine. Current as checked on 17 July 2026.
